message:
feat(ad,api): implement Jacobian and Hessian matrix computation

Add full Jacobian and Hessian support to the automatic differentiation
engine, enabling higher-order derivative analysis of traced Jaxpr
programs.

Jacobian (fj-ad::jacobian_jaxpr):
- Computes the full Jacobian matrix [output_dim x input_dim] via
  forward-mode AD (JVP) with standard basis vector sweeps
- Supports mixed scalar/tensor inputs and outputs with automatic
  flattening and reconstruction
- Row-major layout: each column is one JVP evaluation with a basis
  tangent vector

Hessian (fj-ad::hessian_jaxpr):
- Computes the full Hessian matrix [input_dim x input_dim] for
  scalar-output functions using central-difference approximation
  of the gradient (epsilon=1e-5)
- Symmetric by construction for smooth functions; validated by
  test_hessian_is_symmetric_for_quadratic

Supporting utilities:
- flatten_value_to_f64 / flatten_values_to_f64: convert Value trees
  to flat f64 vectors for numerical operations
- value_from_flat_like / reconstruct_args_from_flat: reconstruct
  typed Values from flat vectors using template shapes
- basis_value_like: generate one-hot basis vectors matching Value shape
- global_basis_to_arg_index: map global basis index to (arg, local)
- matrix_value: construct 2D tensor from flat entries

API layer (fj-api):
- Add JacobianWrapped and HessianWrapped transform types with call()
- Export jacobian() and hessian() constructor functions
- Add From<AdError> for ApiError conversion

Tests:
- jacobian: 2-output 2-input (f(x,y) = [x+y, x*y]) verifies
  [[1,1],[y,x]] structure
- hessian: x²y function verifies [[2y,2x],[2x,0]] at (2,3)
- hessian symmetry: x² + xy quadratic confirms H[0,1] ≈ H[1,0]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
